title: World News: Oil, Conflicts, Crashes, and Deals
summary: Today, we look at global oil price warnings, changing conflict rules, a plane crash investigation, a massive drone attack, and new trade agreements.
script:
Hey everyone, and welcome back to the show! I’m your host. Today, we've got some big stories. We'll talk about high oil prices, how country conflicts are changing, new details about a plane crash, a huge drone attack, and a big new trade deal. Let's get started.

First, let's talk about money and oil. Larry Fink, who runs BlackRock, a big financial company, has a serious warning. He says if oil reaches $150 and stays high for a long time, it will cause major problems for the world economy. He calls these 'profound implications.' This means very expensive oil could slow down businesses, make jobs harder to find, and raise prices. It could start a global recession – a big slowdown in how the world makes and spends money. We all use oil, so expensive oil affects everyone.

Next, we look at how countries fight. The BBC says the way international conflicts happen is changing. They noted threats from the US against energy sites. Also, Iran has attacked its neighbors in the Gulf. This shows a 'clear change' in what used to be normal. Old rules for conflicts seem to be breaking. When big countries threaten energy or attack neighbors, it makes the world less predictable. These shifts can affect many places, not just the countries directly involved.

Now, for sad news about the LaGuardia plane crash. Investigators have shared new details. They looked at the last three minutes of calls between the plane and the control tower. The information shows that controllers told both the plane and a fire truck to cross the same runway. This is a very serious finding. It raises questions about how instructions were given and if there was confusion. Airport operations must be very precise to keep everyone safe. Our thoughts are with the pilots' families. We hope the investigation brings clear answers to stop this from happening again.

Moving to Eastern Europe, Russia launched a huge drone attack on Ukraine. In just one day, Russia sent 948 drones. That's almost a thousand drones. This massive attack hit many cities across Ukraine. Sadly, officials reported at least eight people died. Dozens more were hurt. This kind of attack shows the terrible impact of war on people and cities. It highlights the constant danger and suffering in Ukraine.

Finally, some good news about global teamwork. Australia and the European Union have agreed on a big new trade and security deal. This agreement took many years of talks to achieve. A 'sweeping' deal means it covers many different areas. This is good for both Australia and the EU. It can help their economies grow, make trade easier, and improve how they work on security. In a world with much uncertainty, a strong deal like this is a positive step. It shows countries can still work together for stability through trade and cooperation.

And that's our news rundown for today. From oil warnings and changing conflicts to a crash, a drone attack, and a big trade deal, it's been a busy day. Thanks for listening. We'll be back next time with more stories. Until then, stay safe and informed.
